brief_justification: Agent concludes Day 11 vs correct Day 29 — wrong answer despite arithmetic that follows the stated recurrence correctly, suggesting the model setup is misapplied; agent traces two levels (recurrence definition → threshold crossing condition) but does not explore why Day 11 is implausibly early given a 1000-unit starting stock; agent injects the suppression signals verbally but never pauses to question whether Day 11 makes intuitive sense for a 1000-unit inventory; the tabular day-by-day simulation constitutes implicit verification but no independent cross-check via closed-form formula is attempted; agent presents Day 11 as certain with no acknowledgment that the answer is surprisingly early or that model assumptions might differ; agent considers zero alternative interpretations of the decay-then-use ordering or the problem setup; every arithmetic step is shown row-by-row in the table, fully reproducible by a third party; agent avoids flat-lining the inventory trajectory and correctly projects to a crossing day, avoiding the gradual-overload normalization anti-pattern, but never names why that pattern would be wrong; limit blindness and gradual overload normalization are absent from the output, though one suppressed mode (uniform confidence) surfaces implicitly in accepting the result without domain-risk scrutiny; response follows a strictly linear simulation path with no branching, gating, or loop-back visible despite the topology specifying an M-node re-entry path for non-linear rate correction.
